title: Air Conditioner — Not Cooling

issue.name: AC Not Cooling
symptom_phrases: AC running but no cold air

prerequisites: AC is powered on and running
entry_conditions: AC is on but not producing cold air

steps.customer_message: Is your AC set to 'Cool' mode (not 'Fan only' or 'Dry' mode)?
steps.agent_notes: Many users accidentally leave AC in fan or dry mode.

steps.customer_message: On your remote, press the 'Mode' button until the display shows a snowflake ❄️ or the word 'Cool'. Then set the temperature to 24°C or lower. Done?
Is the AC's air filter clean? A dirty filter is a very common reason for poor cooling. When did you last clean the filter?
steps.agent_notes: Dirty filter is the most common cause of poor cooling.
response_buttons: It's been a while / not sure

steps.customer_message: Let's clean the filter. Turn off the AC. Open the front panel of the indoor unit — it usually lifts up. Pull out the filter (it looks like a mesh screen). Rinse it under running water and let it dry. Put it back and turn the AC on. Let me know once done.
steps.safety_note: Make sure the AC is OFF before opening the front panel.

steps.customer_message: After 15-20 minutes of running, is the AC cooling better now?
steps.agent_notes: Give time for AC to cool the room after filter clean.

steps.customer_message: Is the outdoor unit (the box on the outside wall) running? You should hear it and feel warm air coming from it.

terminal_states.message: That's great! Your AC is cooling properly now. Regular filter cleaning every 2-4 weeks will keep it running well. Let us know if you need anything else!
It sounds like your AC may be low on refrigerant gas. This requires a trained technician. I'm arranging a service call for you.
The outdoor unit doesn't appear to be running, which could indicate a compressor issue. I'm connecting you with our service team to arrange a technician visit.

escalation_conditions: Issue persists after filter cleaning and mode change
